name: Fukurokuju
description: Fukurokuju, often depicted with an extraordinarily elongated head, is one of the Seven Lucky Gods (Shichi Fukujin) in Japanese folklore. He is the god of wisdom, longevity, virility, happiness, and wealth. While his origins are debated, he is believed to be derived from Chinese Taoist figures, specifically a Song dynasty star god associated with the Southern Cross constellation. Fukurokuju is often portrayed as an elderly man with a long white beard, dressed in traditional Chinese robes, and carrying a staff and a scroll. The scroll is said to contain the wisdom of the world. His elongated head is a symbol of his wisdom and longevity, and he is often accompanied by a crane, turtle, or deer, all symbols of long life in East Asian cultures. He is a popular figure in Japanese art and is frequently depicted alongside the other Lucky Gods, representing a harmonious blend of Shinto and Buddhist beliefs adapted from Chinese influences.
subtitle: God of Wisdom and Longevity
titles: One of the Seven Lucky Gods

keyMyths.title: Fukurokuju and the Crane
keyMyths.description: One popular tale recounts how Fukurokuju saved a crane from being harmed by hunters. As a reward for his kindness, the crane transformed into a beautiful woman and brought Fukurokuju great wealth and prosperity. This myth highlights Fukurokuju's benevolent nature and the karmic rewards associated with compassion and protecting life. The crane, a symbol of longevity, further reinforces Fukurokuju's association with a long and prosperous life, blessed by the heavens.

keyMyths.title: The Secret of the Elongated Head
keyMyths.description: The origin of Fukurokuju's elongated head is often explained as a physical manifestation of his accumulated wisdom and knowledge. It is said that his head grew longer as he absorbed more of the world's secrets and learned the mysteries of the universe. This distinctive feature serves as a visual reminder of the importance of seeking knowledge and striving for enlightenment, even into old age. The head symbolizes a vessel overflowing with wisdom.

keyMyths.title: Fukurokuju and the Game of Go
keyMyths.description: Fukurokuju is sometimes associated with the game of Go (Igo). Legend says he possesses exceptional skill at the game. Stories tell of him descending from the heavens to play against mortals, imparting strategic wisdom and insight. This association links him not only to wisdom in a general sense but also to strategic thinking, planning, and foresight, all valuable attributes for a long and successful life. Playing Go with him is considered a great honor.
keyMyths.source: Ancient Japanese Game Records and Folklore
